title: Polygala obversa
intro: Polygala obversa, also known as Yin-Chen-Hao in traditional Chinese medicine, is a type of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to promote urination, reduce edema, and alleviate symptoms of kidney and urinary system disorders. Bioactive constituents of Polygala obversa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polygala obversa is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to treat conditions such as nephritis, urinary tract infections, and edema.
taxonomy: Polygala obversa is classified in the family Polygalaceae, commonly known as the milkwort family. This family comprises approximately 830 species of flowering plants. Polygala obversa is further classified in the genus Polygala, which consists of about 180 species. The genus Polygala is divided into several subgenera, each with distinct characteristics. Polygala obversa is native to China and is considered a rare and endangered species.
uses: Polygala obversa, also known as the Variable Milkwort, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The plant is also said to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds. Additionally, it may help in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.
